title: carex remota subsp. alta
intro: Carex remota subsp. alta, also known as the tall sedge, is a subspecies of perennial sedge plant native to the western coast of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Carex remota subsp. alta include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially helping to manage symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the rhizomes and roots being the primary parts used for medicinal purposes.
